title: Personal Introduction
content: Pavan Kumar Kunukuntla is an AI Engineer with hands-on experience building LLM-powered applications, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems, multi-agent workflows, and scalable AI backends. He is currently a final year B.Tech student (4th Year) at Rajiv Gandhi University of Knowledge Technologies (RGUKT) Basar, pursuing Computer Science and Engineering in a 6-Year Integrated program from 2021 to 2027. He is based in Basar, Telangana, India. Contact: pavankumarkunukuntla@gmail.com Phone: +91 9391118474

title: Professional Summary and Passion
content: Pavan is passionate about transforming cutting-edge AI research into practical high-impact products. He continuously explores agentic AI, fine-tuning, and autonomous systems. He is proficient in Python, FastAPI, LangChain, LangGraph, vector databases, and modern AI frameworks. He has developed end-to-end solutions for semantic search, conversational AI, knowledge extraction, and multimodal systems integrating language, vision, and speech models. His strong foundation covers machine learning, data processing, model evaluation, and production AI system design.

title: Agentic AI and RAG Frameworks
content: Pavan's core expertise is Agentic AI. He specializes in Multi-Agent Orchestration, RAG Pipelines, LangChain, LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, and MCP (Model Context Protocol). He works extensively with Vector Databases including FAISS, ChromaDB, Pinecone, and Qdrant. He has built retrieval memory systems and complex multi-agent state machines using LangGraph StateGraph with type-safe state tracking and cyclic retry loops.

title: Multimodal AI and Vision Systems
content: Pavan has strong expertise in Multimodal AI systems. He works with Vision-Language Models, CLIP, BLIP, EasyOCR, and Stability AI SDXL for image generation. He implements Multimodal Embeddings and uses BGE Reranker and Cross-Encoder Rerankers to improve retrieval quality in RAG systems. He has built systems that fuse visual and text embeddings for improved diagnostic accuracy over unimodal baselines.

title: Machine Learning NLP and Deep Learning
content: Pavan is skilled in Machine Learning and NLP with experience in TensorFlow, Scikit-learn, BiLSTM, Llama 3, Embeddings, and Semantic Similarity. He has worked on Model Evaluation, SLM Fine-tuning, and Reinforcement Learning concepts. His NLP work includes semantic search, text classification, and knowledge extraction from unstructured data. He also has Data Science skills including NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, Feature Engineering, EDA, Data Preprocessing, and Statistical Analysis.

title: Backend Data Engineering and DevOps
content: Pavan builds production-grade backends using Django, Flask, FastAPI, and Node.js. He designs REST APIs with JWT Authentication and uses Celery for async task processing and SSE for streaming. His database expertise spans PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Firestore, Supabase, Neo4j, and Redis. He is proficient in ETL Pipelines, SQL optimization, and schema design. For DevOps he uses Git, Docker, Vercel, Firebase, and Linux. He codes in Python, Java, TypeScript, and JavaScript.

title: FailureRAG — Self-Learning Self-Healing RAG
content: FailureRAG is a self-learning and self-healing RAG system. Pavan built a 9-agent LangGraph StateGraph engine with type-safe state tracking and cyclic retry loops for autonomous error recovery. He designed pre-generation quality gates scoring retrieved evidence on Relevance, Completeness, Freshness, Calibration, and Contradiction before token generation. Automated root-cause failure diagnosis with real-time query reformulation and live PubMed fetches. Integrated Qdrant for hybrid dense/sparse search, Neo4j for citation graph traversal, Redis for SimHash cache, and Supabase for telemetry. Parallelized hot-path calls via asyncio.gather cutting streaming latency 45 percent from 3.5s to 1.9s. Reduced cold-start RAM 93 percent from 650MB to 42MB using lazy-loaded embeddings. Tech Stack: Python, FastAPI, LangGraph, Qdrant, Neo4j AuraDB, Redis, Supabase, Gemini 2.0 Flash, Celery.

title: Nina — AI Voice Navigation SDK
content: Nina is an AI Voice Navigation SDK built in 2025. Pavan shipped a single script-tag voice SDK enabling natural-language navigation across any web application, reducing user interaction steps by 50 percent. Devised a hybrid inference pipeline: deterministic fast-path of approximately 5 milliseconds handles 60 percent of queries while Llama 3.1 resolves ambiguous multi-step commands. Enabled multi-step command execution with contextual memory, task chaining, and session-aware navigation across 4 web platforms. Deployed multi-tenant SaaS architecture with SHA-256 API key hashing, Row Level Security, and Supabase Realtime live dashboard. Incorporated DOM-aware navigation for dynamic UI interaction including form filling and page routing. Tech Stack: TypeScript SDK, FastAPI, Groq API with Llama 3.1, Supabase.

title: TaxSetu — Multi-Agent GST Compliance Platform
content: TaxSetu is a multi-agent GST compliance platform built for India, submitted at KSUM Build For India hackathon in February 2026. Pavan engineered a 5-agent architecture covering OCR ingestion, GST computation, ITC reconciliation, compliance validation, and anomaly detection cutting manual review effort by 70 percent. Orchestrated inter-agent communication via structured JSON outputs with decision, reasoning, confidence, and notify flags for fully traceable audit-ready workflows. Built a multimodal ingestion pipeline handling scanned invoices, CSV and Excel uploads, and voice transcripts with automated GSTIN validation and ledger mapping. Delivered LLM-assisted anomaly detection flagging duplicate invoices and ITC mismatches. Launched multilingual voice-first accessibility layer for the 88 percent of Indian MSMEs underserved by English-only platforms. Tech Stack: React, Vite, Tailwind CSS, Firebase, Gemini API, Node.js, Cloud Functions.

title: VisionSync — Agentic Film Pre-Production
content: VisionSync is an agentic film pre-production platform that won 6th place at the CineAI Hackathon at T-Works Hyderabad in January 2026. Pavan architected a 5-stage agent pipeline for screenplay analysis covering story structure, genre classification, character arcs, and visual continuity validation. Used vision-language models to evaluate scene-level continuity reducing manual storyboard review by 60 percent. Produced structured production breakdowns with shot lists, scene metadata, character maps, and location data. Introduced production feasibility scoring across scene complexity, budget estimation, and scheduling constraints. Embedded Stability AI SDXL to generate storyboard visuals directly from screenplay text. Reduced screenplay-to-production turnaround from days to minutes. Tech Stack: React, TypeScript, Express, Firestore, Gemini Vision, Stability AI SDXL.

title: AI Livestock Health Assistant — Multimodal RAG
content: Pavan designed a multimodal AI system for livestock disease detection using combined image and symptom inputs across cattle, goats, sheep, and buffalo. Implemented Multimodal RAG by fusing CLIP visual embeddings with text embeddings to improve diagnostic accuracy over unimodal baselines. Built a hybrid retrieval pipeline integrating HNSW vector search via ChromaDB and BM25 keyword search with metadata filtering for high-confidence disease retrieval. Applied cross-encoder reranking using BGE Reranker to refine top-k retrieved results. Developed a confidence scoring system combining image similarity, text similarity, and symptom matching. Integrated LLM reasoning using Llama 3 locally with Gemini as fallback, with medical guardrails avoiding direct prescriptions and recommending veterinary consultation for high-risk cases. Tech Stack: Python, FastAPI, ChromaDB, CLIP, BM25, BGE Reranker, Llama 3, Gemini API, Streamlit.

title: AI and Data Pipeline Intern at Microsoft Elevate
content: Pavan interned as an AI and Data Pipeline Intern at Microsoft Elevate from December 2025 to January 2026. He formulated an end-to-end data ingestion pipeline for healthcare datasets covering structured records and semi-structured sources, supporting 5 or more downstream analytics workflows. Conducted preprocessing, feature extraction, and normalization across 10 or more dataset variants to prepare data for machine learning and semantic analysis. Established an embedding-based semantic analytics layer enabling similarity search and pattern discovery, reducing manual data exploration time by 35 percent. Optimized query execution and processing workflows, reducing pipeline latency by 40 percent. Crafted analytics-ready schema improving reporting consistency for 3 or more teams.

title: Python Full Stack Intern at Infosys Springboard
content: Pavan interned as a Python Full Stack Intern at Infosys Springboard from November 2025 to January 2026. He rolled out a Django full-stack application with 20 or more REST API endpoints and PostgreSQL backend for enterprise workflow automation. Planned a normalised database schema across 8 or more tables optimized for analytics and reporting. Enforced role-based access control across 3 user tiers with structured approval workflow management. Instituted an audit logging system tracking all user activity and data modifications across multi-step operations. Reduced manual processing by 30 percent through expanded API coverage with CRUD endpoints.

title: B.Tech Computer Science at RGUKT Basar
content: Pavan is pursuing a B.Tech 6-Year Integrated program in Computer Science and Engineering at Rajiv Gandhi University of Knowledge Technologies, RGUKT, Basar from 2021 to 2027. He is currently in his Final Year which is 4th Year B.Tech. He achieved a PUC CGPA of 9.50 and a B.Tech CGPA of 8.93 up to 3rd Year. RGUKT is a premier institution in Telangana, India focused on engineering and technology education for rural students.

content: Pavan holds the following professional certifications. IBM RAG and Agentic AI Professional Certificate from IBM on Coursera. Machine Learning Specialization from DeepLearning.AI on Coursera. Deep Learning Specialization from DeepLearning.AI on Coursera. These certifications demonstrate strong theoretical and practical foundation in modern AI, RAG systems, agentic AI, and deep learning from globally recognized institutions.

title: Hackathon Wins and Competitions
content: Pavan has a strong hackathon track record. He won the Smart India Hackathon Internal round at RGUKT Basar in 2025. He secured 6th Place at the CineAI Hackathon organized by Lorven AI at T-Works Hyderabad in January 2026 where he built VisionSync. He participated in Build For India organized by KSUM in Kochi Kerala in February 2026 where he built TaxSetu the GST compliance platform. He also participated in the TuteDude Hackathon online in 2025.

content: Pavan Kumar Kunukuntla is currently open to AI engineering internships, freelance projects, and full-time opportunities after his graduation in 2027. content: What sets Pavan apart is his deep focus on Agentic AI and building systems that think, plan, and act autonomously. He does not just use AI APIs but architects complete multi-agent systems with complex state machines, self-healing pipelines, and production-grade reliability. He combines full-stack engineering skills in React and FastAPI and PostgreSQL with deep AI expertise in LangGraph, RAG, and multimodal systems. content: Pavan is an advanced AI engineer despite being a final year student. He has real-world internship experience at Microsoft Elevate and Infosys Springboard. content: Pavan Kumar Kunukuntla completed his early schooling and secondary education in Telangana, India. In this integrated model, he completed his Pre-University Course, PUC, with an outstanding CGPA of 9.50, before choosing Computer Science and Engineering for his B.Tech degree, where he has achieved a CGPA of 8.93.

content: Outside of structured engineering and academics, Pavan is a builder at heart who enjoys prototyping new open-source AI utilities. He frequently reads AI research papers on arXiv to stay updated on LLM breakthroughs, watches technical podcasts such as Lex Fridman and Latent Space, contributes to developer forums, and plays strategic video games that challenge his logical reasoning.

content: Pavan is deeply interested in the evolution of Agentic AI and cognitive architecture. His core research interests lie in Multi-Agent coordination, Retrieval-Augmented Generation, RAG, optimization, Model Context Protocol, MCP, and spatial or embodied intelligence. He is passionate about making AI systems reliable and self-healing, which led him to build projects like FailureRAG. He constantly explores how model fine-tuning and reinforcement learning can improve local reasoning capabilities.

content: Pavan exhibits a proactive, execution-oriented, and curious personality. He possesses a strong builder mindset, preferring to learn new technologies by immediately building projects with them. He is collaborative, open to feedback, and values clear, transparent communication. His experience leading teams at hackathons highlights his leadership, adaptability, and high resilience when facing complex debug sessions under tight deadlines.

content: Pavan is a fast learner with a proven track record of picking up complex systems rapidly. For instance, during the CineAI and KSUM hackathons, he mastered new tools like Gemini Vision, Stability AI, and custom voice integrations within a 24-to-48 hour window to build and deploy award-winning applications. His project FailureRAG demonstrates his ability to independently grasp and combine advanced concepts such as LangGraph StateGraph, Neo4j graph databases, Redis caches, and Celery task queues in a very short timeframe.
